Cleaner.description: Reduces the target persona's Overwatch Score by 1 for each hit scored. The form only affects existing Overwatch Score and does not prevent new score from accumulating.

Diffusion of Attack.description: Reduces the target's Attack attribute by the technomancer's net hits for as long as the form is sustained. The attribute cannot be reduced below 1.

Diffusion of Sleaze.description: Reduces the target's Sleaze attribute by the technomancer's net hits for as long as the form is sustained. The attribute cannot be reduced below 1.

Diffusion of Data Processing.description: Reduces the target's Data Processing attribute by the technomancer's net hits for as long as the form is sustained. The attribute cannot be reduced below 1.

Diffusion of Firewall.description: Reduces the target's Firewall attribute by the technomancer's net hits for as long as the form is sustained. The attribute cannot be reduced below 1.

Editor.test: Software + Resonance [Level] v. Intuition + Data Processing of the file's owner
Editor.description: Infuses a file with Resonance and lets the technomancer manipulate its contents. The number of net hits determines how many changes can be made, using the same scale as Edit File actions.

Infusion of Attack.test: Simple Software + Resonance [Level] Test; Level must equal or exceed the current attribute rating
Infusion of Attack.description: Boosts the target's Attack attribute by the hits scored, up to twice its normal rating. Only one Infusion can affect a given attribute at a time, and the form ends if that boosted attribute is swapped through Reconfigure.

Infusion of Sleaze.test: Simple Software + Resonance [Level] Test; Level must equal or exceed the current attribute rating
Infusion of Sleaze.description: Boosts the target's Sleaze attribute by the hits scored, up to twice its normal rating. Only one Infusion can affect a given attribute at a time, and the form ends if that boosted attribute is swapped through Reconfigure.

Infusion of Data Processing.test: Simple Software + Resonance [Level] Test; Level must equal or exceed the current attribute rating
Infusion of Data Processing.description: Boosts the target's Data Processing attribute by the hits scored, up to twice its normal rating. Only one Infusion can affect a given attribute at a time, and the form ends if that boosted attribute is swapped through Reconfigure.

Infusion of Firewall.test: Simple Software + Resonance [Level] Test; Level must equal or exceed the current attribute rating
Infusion of Firewall.description: Boosts the target's Firewall attribute by the hits scored, up to twice its normal rating. Only one Infusion can affect a given attribute at a time, and the form ends if that boosted attribute is swapped through Reconfigure.

Static Veil.test: Simple Software + Resonance [Level] Test; threshold 1 on the public grid or 2 on any other grid
Static Veil.description: While sustained and while the target remains on the same grid, the target's Overwatch Score does not increase from the passage of time. Illegal actions can still increase Overwatch Score normally.

Pulse Storm.test: Software + Resonance [Level] v. Logic + Data Processing
Pulse Storm.description: Floods the target with disruptive signals. Each net hit increases the target's Noise by 1.

Puppeteer.test: Software + Resonance [Level] v. Willpower + Firewall; threshold 1 for a Free Action, 2 for a Simple Action, or 3 for a Complex Action
Puppeteer.description: Forces the target to perform a chosen Matrix action as its next available action. The required threshold depends on the action type being forced.

Resonance Channel.description: Creates a Resonance channel to the target. Each hit reduces Noise caused by distance from that target by 1 while the form is sustained.

Resonance Spike.description: Sends destructive Resonance into the target. The target suffers 1 box of Matrix damage per net hit, with no additional damage-resistance test.

Resonance Veil.test: Software + Resonance [Level] v. Intuition + Data Processing
Resonance Veil.description: Creates a convincing Matrix illusion for the target. A suspicious target must succeed on a Matrix Perception Test with a threshold equal to the technomancer's net hits to see through it.

Static Bomb.test: Software + Resonance [Level] v. Intuition + Data Processing of each icon that has spotted the technomancer
Static Bomb.description: Attempts to break observation by every icon that has spotted the technomancer. Each icon beaten by the opposed test stops spotting the technomancer, except an icon that already has a mark on the technomancer.

Stitches.description: Repairs a sprite's Resonance pattern. Remove 1 box of Matrix damage from the target sprite for each hit scored.

Transcendent Grid.description: Connects the technomancer to all grids at once. The technomancer suffers no cross-grid or public-grid penalty, while other icons targeting the technomancer also suffer no cross-grid penalty. The effect lasts 1 minute per hit.

Tattletale.description: Mimics the traces of illegal Matrix activity. Each hit increases the target persona's Overwatch Score by 1, but the form only works on a target that already has an Overwatch Score.

Brute Force.description: Illegally places a mark by force. One success places one mark; attempting two marks in one action applies −4 dice and three marks applies −10 dice. The action may also inflict 1 box of Matrix damage for every 2 full net hits, and can be used to hop illegally to a grid by opposing 4 dice for a local grid or 6 dice for a global grid.

Change Icon.description: Changes the appearance of an icon to a design the user has created or copied. The cosmetic change does not alter the information revealed by Matrix Perception.

Check Overwatch Score.description: Checks how close the persona is to convergence. On success, the gamemaster reveals the Overwatch Score from before the action, then adds the opposing test's hits to the score because this is a Sleaze action.

Control Device.test: As the attempted action [Data Processing or normal limit], or Electronic Warfare + Intuition [Sleaze] v. Intuition + Firewall when the device action normally has no test
Control Device.description: Remotely performs an action through a controlled device. The action type matches the action being attempted. It normally requires 1 mark for a Free Action, 2 marks for a Simple Action, and 3 marks for a Standard or Complex Action. Multiple owned devices can receive the same command together; otherwise the dice pool must be split.

Crack File.description: Removes protection from a file so it can be read and otherwise manipulated normally.

Crash Program.description: Crashes one specified program currently running on the target. On success, the program ends and cannot be restarted until the device running it reboots.

Data Spike.description: Inflicts Matrix damage with a base Damage Value equal to the attacker's Attack rating, plus 1 per net hit and +2 for each mark the attacker has on the target. The target resists with Device Rating + Firewall.

Disarm Data Bomb.test: Software + Intuition [Firewall] v. Data Bomb Rating × 2
Disarm Data Bomb.description: Attempts to remove a detected Data Bomb. Any net hits delete the bomb; failure causes the bomb to activate and resolve normally.

Edit File.test: Computer + Logic [Data Processing] v. Intuition + Firewall
Edit File.description: Creates, changes, copies, deletes, or protects a file. One action normally changes one meaningful detail; continuous edits require one action per Combat Turn. Copying a protected file fails, and copying a file with a Data Bomb triggers it. An owner can set protection with a Simple Computer + Logic [Data Processing] Test, using hits as the protection rating.

Enter/Exit Host.description: Enters a host on which the persona has a mark, or exits the current host. Leaving returns the persona to the grid from which it entered.

Erase Mark.description: Removes a mark from the user's persona or another icon. Erasing a mark from another icon requires three marks on that icon. Two marks can be erased at −4 dice or three at −10 dice, provided they are on the same icon and came from the same source.

Erase Matrix Signature.description: Erases a Matrix signature left by a Resonance being such as a technomancer or sprite. The user must have a Resonance rating. This is an Attack Matrix action and therefore carries the usual Attack-action risks.

Format Device.description: Rewrites a device's boot code so its next reboot shuts it down until its software is replaced with an Extended Software + Logic [Mental] (12, 1 hour) Test. The disabled device loses wireless functionality but may still work as a purely mechanical object.

Full Matrix Defense.description: Reduces Initiative Score by 10 and adds Willpower to every defense test against Matrix actions for the rest of the Combat Turn, adding it again when Willpower is already part of the normal defense pool.

Grid Hop.description: Moves the persona to another grid for which it has legitimate access. Illegal grid access requires Brute Force or Hack on the Fly. A persona must leave a host before changing grids.

Hack on the Fly.description: Illegally places marks through stealth. One success places one mark; attempting two marks applies −4 dice and three marks applies −10 dice. Every 2 full net hits also count as 1 hit on a Matrix Perception Test. The action can also hop illegally to a grid by opposing 4 dice for a local grid or 6 dice for a global grid.

Hide.test: Electronic Warfare + Intuition [Sleaze] v. Intuition + Data Processing
Hide.description: Makes a chosen icon stop spotting the user on success. The target must use Matrix Perception to find the user again. An icon that has a mark on the user cannot be hidden from until that mark is removed.

Invite Mark.description: Allows an owner to offer another icon permission to place marks. The owner chooses the number of marks allowed, their duration, and how long the invitation remains open. The invitee accepts by placing the mark with a Free Action.

Jack Out.test: Hardware + Willpower [Firewall] v. Logic + Attack when link-locked; otherwise no opposed test
Jack Out.description: Disconnects the user from the Matrix and reboots the device being used. A user in VR suffers dumpshock. If link-locked, the user must beat every icon maintaining a link-lock using one roll compared against each opponent separately.

Jam Signals.description: Turns the user's wireless device into a local jammer. Hits add to the Noise rating for Matrix actions made by or targeting devices within 100 meters. The effect ends when the user performs another Matrix action with that device.

Jump Into Rigged Device.test: Electronic Warfare + Logic [Data Processing] v. Willpower + Firewall
Jump Into Rigged Device.description: Jumps the user into a rigger-adapted device. The user must be in VR, have a control rig, and have three marks on the device. The test is unnecessary for the owner or a user with the owner's permission. A device already occupied by another jumped-in user cannot be entered.

Matrix Perception.test: Computer + Intuition [Data Processing]; opposed by Logic + Sleaze when spotting a silent-running icon
Matrix Perception.description: Finds and analyzes Matrix icons. Each net hit can reveal one piece of information about an analyzed object. Spotting a distant icon requires at least one hit; spotting a silent-running icon uses an opposed test, with the first net hit spotting the target and additional net hits providing information.

Matrix Search.test: Simple Computer + Intuition [Data Processing] Test; threshold and base time depend on the information sought
Matrix Search.description: Searches the Matrix for information. General or public information is threshold 1 with a 1-minute base time; limited or unpublicized information is threshold 3 with 30 minutes; hidden or actively erased information is threshold 6 with 12 hours. Hits beyond the threshold reduce the search time. Protected or secret information normally requires access to the host storing it.

Reboot Device.test: Computer + Logic [Data Processing] v. Willpower + Firewall
Reboot Device.description: Shuts down and reboots a device, which normally returns at the end of the following Combat Turn. Rebooting the user's own persona resets Overwatch Score to 0 and clears all marks on that icon. The action only works on devices and the user's own persona, cannot target a link-locked device, and requires no test when used by the owner.

Send Message.description: Sends a short text or audio sentence, image, or file to a user whose commcode is known. With DNI, a longer message of roughly a paragraph can be sent, and the action can also open a live feed to one or more recipients.

Set Data Bomb.description: Places a Data Bomb in a file with a rating up to the net hits scored. The setter chooses a passcode and whether activation deletes the file. Interacting with the file without the passcode triggers Rating D6 Matrix damage, may delete the file, and removes the bomb.

Snoop.test: Electronic Warfare + Intuition [Sleaze] v. Logic + Firewall
Snoop.description: Intercepts Matrix traffic sent to and from the marked target. The intercepted data can be monitored live or stored for later playback if the user has storage available.

Spoof Command.description: Impersonates a device owner's authority and sends a command that appears legitimate. The user needs one mark on the icon being imitated but no mark on the target. This works on devices and agents, not IC, sprites, hosts, personas, or other icon types.

Switch Interface Mode.description: Switches the user's perception between AR and VR. Switching into VR leaves the body limp; switching back to AR removes VR Initiative Dice. A link-locked user cannot change interface mode, and the action can only target the user.

Trace Icon.test: Computer + Intuition [Data Processing] v. Willpower + Sleaze
Trace Icon.description: Finds the physical location of a device or persona. After success, the location remains known while the tracer retains at least one mark on the target. The action does not work on hosts or IC programs.

category.Complex Forms:
Complex Forms
Technomancers use the Thread Complex Form Resonance action to create learned Resonance effects. A form is threaded with Software + Resonance [Level], choosing a Level up to three times the technomancer's Resonance. Only spotted icons can be targeted. Sustained forms impose a −2 dice pool penalty to all actions per form sustained. Fading is resisted with Resonance + Willpower; it is Physical if the threading test scores more hits than the technomancer's Resonance rating, otherwise Stun. A complex form's Fading DV can never be lower than 2.
category.Matrix Actions:
Matrix Actions
Matrix actions are actions performed in the Matrix and are affected by rules such as Noise, marks, and Overwatch Score. Tests use the listed Matrix attribute as their limit. Attack and Sleaze actions are illegal and can increase Overwatch Score; failed Attack actions can also cause Matrix damage to the attacker. When a defense test calls for a Mental attribute belonging to an unattended device, use its Device Rating in place of that attribute.

subcategories.Attribute Manipulation:
Attribute Manipulation
An organisational grouping for Core Rulebook complex forms that directly reduce or increase one of the four Matrix attributes: Attack, Sleaze, Data Processing, or Firewall. Diffusion lowers the chosen attribute through an opposed test, while Infusion raises it subject to the form's Level and the target's normal rating.
subcategories.Overwatch Manipulation:
Overwatch Manipulation
An organisational grouping for complex forms that alter or suppress Overwatch Score. Cleaner reduces existing score, Static Veil prevents time-based accumulation while sustained under its conditions, and Tattletale increases an existing score.
subcategories.Resonance File Manipulation:
Resonance File Manipulation
An organisational grouping for complex forms that directly manipulate Matrix files through Resonance rather than through the normal Edit File action.
subcategories.Matrix Influence:
Matrix Influence
An organisational grouping for complex forms that alter what a target perceives or compel a target device to take an action.
subcategories.Matrix Offense:
Matrix Offense
An organisational grouping for complex forms that directly harm or disrupt another Matrix user or device through Matrix damage or Noise.
subcategories.Signal & Access:
Signal & Access
An organisational grouping for complex forms that manipulate visibility, distance-based Noise, or grid access.
subcategories.Sprite Support:
Sprite Support
An organisational grouping for complex forms that directly repair or otherwise aid sprites.
subcategories.Device Manipulation:
Device Manipulation
The Core Rulebook's Matrix-action function group for remotely controlling devices, formatting their boot code, rebooting them, or spoofing commands from an apparent owner.
subcategories.File Manipulation:
File Manipulation
The Core Rulebook's Matrix-action function group for removing file protection, disarming Data Bombs, editing files, and setting Data Bombs.
subcategories.Information Gathering:
Information Gathering
The Core Rulebook's Matrix-action function group for checking Overwatch Score, perceiving icons, searching for information, intercepting Matrix traffic, and tracing physical locations.
subcategories.Mark Manipulation:
Mark Manipulation
The Core Rulebook's Matrix-action function group for gaining, removing, or inviting marks and for manipulating Resonance signatures.
subcategories.Matrix Combat:
Matrix Combat
The Core Rulebook's Matrix-action function group for crashing programs, inflicting Matrix damage, and improving Matrix defenses during a Combat Turn.
subcategories.Miscellaneous:
Miscellaneous
The Core Rulebook's catch-all Matrix-action group for actions such as changing icons, entering or leaving hosts, changing grids, hiding, jacking out, jamming, jumping into rigged devices, sending messages, and switching interface modes.
